name: Rose bengal
description: Rose bengal is a stain with a pink color. Its disodium salt form is commonly available in eye drops as a diagnostic agent when there is suspected damage to conjunctival and corneal cells. It is also used in laboratory settings, including the preparation of Foraminifera for microscopic analysis and suppression of bacterial growth in several microbiological media [Wikipedia]. The clinical applications of rose bengal as injectable formulation under the name PV-10 in melanoma, breast cancer and skin conditions such as eczema and psoriasis are being investigated in clinical trials.
